name: foundry
context: Deploy Solidity contracts using Foundry forge scripts with Solidity-native deployment logic
info: Foundry is a Rust-based Ethereum toolchain. Deployment uses `forge script` which executes Solidity scripts that call `vm.broadcast()` to send transactions. Foundry is faster than Hardhat for compilation and testing. Deployment scripts live in `script/` and use the `Script` base contract. Foundry tracks deployments via broadcast logs in `broadcast/`. It supports `--verify` for automatic Etherscan verification and `--resume` for recovering interrupted deployments.

sections.title: Foundry setup
sections.body: Install with `curl -L https://foundry.paradigm.xyz | bash && foundryup`. Init project with `forge init`. Source goes in `src/`, tests in `test/`, scripts in `script/`.

sections.title: Forge script deployment
sections.body: Deployment scripts extend `Script` from forge-std. Use `vm.startBroadcast(key)` / `vm.stopBroadcast()` to wrap deployment calls. Run with `forge script <path> --rpc-url <url> --broadcast`. Add `--verify` for auto-verification (see [[ethereum/contract-deploy/verify#foundry-verify]]). Broadcast logs go to `broadcast/`. Configure the deployer key per [[ethereum/wallets#key-storage]].

sections.title: Quick deploy with forge create
sections.body: `forge create src/Contract.sol:Contract --rpc-url $RPC_URL --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY --constructor-args arg1 arg2`. Simpler than scripts but not repeatable. Good for one-off deploys.

links.context: Create the deployer wallet for forge scripts
Detailed verification steps with forge verify-contract

related.note: Hardhat is the JavaScript-based alternative to Foundry

scripts.name: Foundry Deploy
scripts.description: Deploy a contract using forge script
